title: Per capita electricity generation from fossil fuels
caption:
Per capita electricity generation from fossil fuels, 2025
Electricity generation from coal, oil, and gas, measured in kilowatt-hours per person.

This choropleth map displays the global distribution of electricity generated from fossil fuels per person in 2025, with values measured in kilowatt-hours per person. The color scale ranges from light beige for low generation (0 to 100 kWh) to dark brown for the highest generation (up to 10,000 kWh), with hatched areas indicating no available data. The highest values, falling in the 5,000 to 10,000 kWh band, are concentrated in the Middle East, specifically Saudi Arabia, and across North America, including the United States and Canada. Australia and Russia also appear in the darker orange bands, indicating generation between 2,000 and 5,000 kWh per person. In contrast, the lowest values, in the 0 to 100 kWh band, cover most of sub-Saharan Africa and parts of South Asia. Large regions of South America and Southeast Asia fall into the intermediate bands of 100 to 500 kWh. Western Europe shows a mix of mid-range values, generally between 500 and 2,000 kWh. The map shows a clear geographic divide where per capita fossil fuel electricity generation is highest in resource-rich nations and industrialized economies, while remaining lowest across the African continent.
